originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Big News from Cameroon: A Very Old Leader Gets a Helper!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly reporter from Africa is here with some exciting news! Today, we are going to talk about a very special country called Cameroon, which is right here in our amazing Africa. In Cameroon, there is a very important person called the President. His name is President Paul Biya. He is very, very old! How old, you ask? He is 93 years old! Can you imagine being 93? That's older than most of our grandmas and grandpas, and even older than some great-grandmas and great-grandpas! He has been the leader of Cameroon for a super long time, 43 years! And guess what? For the first time in all those years, he is getting a special helper called a 'deputy'. Let's find out more!

Meet President Paul Biya

President Paul Biya is like the head teacher of the whole country of Cameroon. He has a very important job looking after all the people and making sure things work well. Imagine being in charge of all the roads, all the schools, and all the hospitals in a whole country! That's a lot of work, isn't it? He has been doing this job for 43 whole years. That's a super, super long time. If you started school when you were 5, and then went to high school, then college, you still wouldn't have been in school for 43 years! He has seen many, many changes happen in the world and in Cameroon during his time as President. He loves his country very much and works hard for its people.

Where is Cameroon?

Let's take a quick trip to Cameroon! It's a beautiful country in Central Africa. If you look at a map of Africa, you will see it there, shaped a bit like a triangle. It has big forests, tall mountains, and even pretty beaches along the Atlantic Ocean. Many different animals live there, like elephants with their big ears, and strong gorillas! The capital city of Cameroon is called Yaoundé. People in Cameroon speak many languages, including French and English. It's a very diverse place, full of different cultures and traditions. Isn't Africa amazing with all its different countries? **Did you know?** Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different landscapes and cultures, just like our whole continent!

A Leader for a Very Long Time

As we said, President Biya has been the leader for 43 years! Think about this: when he first became President, many of your parents were not even born yet! He has led his country through many years, like a captain guiding a big ship. Last year, the people of Cameroon voted, and he won the big vote again. So, he is still the President. It's a very big responsibility to be the leader for such a long time, and it means he has a lot of experience. He knows the country and its people very well. He has probably made many friends around the world too, because he has been a leader for so long.

What is a "Deputy"?

Now, let's talk about this special new helper. The helper is called a 'deputy'. What does a deputy do? Imagine your teacher in school. Sometimes, your teacher might ask a student to be a 'class monitor' or a 'helper' for the day. That helper does special jobs, like collecting books or helping tidy up. A deputy for a President is a bit like that, but for a whole country! A deputy is someone who can help the President with his big jobs. They can go to meetings, help make plans, and sometimes even stand in for the President when he is busy or needs a rest. It's like having a very trusted assistant or a second-in-command. **Did you know?** The word "deputy" means someone who is chosen to help or take the place of another person!

Why Get a Helper Now?

President Biya has been doing this big job all by himself for 43 years. That's a long, long time to work without a special helper right by your side doing the same kind of work. Maybe he has so many big things to do now that he needs an extra pair of hands and eyes. When you have a big job, it's always good to have someone to share the load. Getting a deputy means that the work of leading Cameroon can be shared. This can help make sure that everything gets done smoothly and that the President has someone important to talk to about all the big decisions. It’s a bit like when you play a team sport, and you have different players working together to win. **Did you know?** Even the strongest leaders sometimes need help. It's smart to ask for help when you have a lot to do!

Good Leaders Need Good Teams

This news about President Biya getting a deputy reminds us of something very important: even the most powerful people need help! No one can do everything alone. Whether it's in your home, in your school, or in a big country, teamwork makes everything better. When a leader has a good team around them, they can make even better decisions for everyone. The deputy will work with President Biya to help Cameroon grow and stay strong. It's like having two good drivers in a car, making sure the journey is safe and smooth for everyone inside. It shows that it's important to share responsibilities and trust others to help.

Learning from This Story

So, what have we learned today? We learned about President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is a very old leader at 93 years old, and has led his country for a super long time, 43 years! We also learned that he is getting a deputy, a special helper, for the very first time. This helper will make sure the big job of leading Cameroon is shared. It reminds us that helping each other is always a good idea, no matter how old or how important you are. Teamwork helps everyone, from a small class project to leading a whole country. Keep being good helpers and good team players, my friends! See you next time!
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is 93 years old and has been the country's leader for 43 years, is getting a special helper called a 'deputy' for the very first time.
whyMatters: This is important because even very important leaders need help, and having a deputy means the big job of leading Cameroon can be shared to make sure everything runs well for the people.

originalTitle: Rodent infestations and attacks spread in Gaza tent camps
title: Tiny Pests, Big Problem: Keeping Safe in Tent Homes
content:
Hello, Super Friends in Africa!

Hello, my wonderful friends all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share a story from a place far, far away. Sometimes, stories can be a little bit sad, but they also teach us how to be strong and help each other. Today, we're going to talk about some tiny animals causing a big worry for families living in special homes called 'tent camps'.

What are Tent Camps?

Imagine if you had to leave your house very quickly because it wasn't safe anymore. Many families in a place called Gaza have had to do this. Gaza is a place far, far away from most of our homes in Africa. When people leave their homes, they often need a new place to stay. So, they live in big tents, all close together. These places are called 'tent camps'. It's like a very big camping trip, but it’s not for fun or holidays. It's where people try to stay safe and live until they can go back home. There are many, many tents, line after line. Lots of children, just like you, live there with their mummies and daddies, brothers and sisters. They try to make their tents feel like a home.

Who are the Tiny Pests?

Now, when lots of people live together in a small area, sometimes tiny animals come to visit. These little animals are called 'rats' or 'mice'. You might have seen them sometimes near dustbins or in fields. They are small, quick, and usually grey or brown. They have little pointy noses and long tails. They are not like our friendly pets, like dogs or cats. Did you know? Rats are very smart animals! They can learn new things quickly, and they are very good at finding food.

Why are There So Many Rats?

In the tent camps in Gaza, many families have to live very close to each other. It's sometimes hard to keep everything super clean all the time. Imagine if you have food, and you finish eating, but some crumbs fall on the floor. Or maybe the rubbish bin gets full very quickly. Well, rats love these things! They are always looking for food. If there are bits of food on the ground, or rubbish piles up, it's like a big party invitation for them. They smell the food and come running. They can find tiny holes to squeeze through and come into the tents. When many, many tents are together, and it's hard to keep all the areas perfectly clean, more and more rats come. This is what we call an 'infestation' – it means there are too many of them!

What Problems Do Rats Cause?

These tiny pests can cause big problems for the families. First, rats like to eat people's food. They might sneak into tents at night and eat food that families have saved. This means less food for the children and their families, which is very serious. Second, rats can carry tiny, tiny germs. These germs can make people sick. Imagine a little rat running over your food, and then you eat it – you might get a tummy ache or feel unwell. This is why it's very important to keep rats away from where we eat and sleep. Did you know? Rats have very strong teeth! They can chew through many things, even some hard plastic containers, to get to food. Sometimes, if a rat gets scared, or if it's very hungry, it might even bite a person, especially children who are sleeping. This can hurt and can also pass on germs, making it even more important to find a way to make them go away.

Helping to Keep Homes Safe

So, what can people do in the tent camps to try and solve this problem? It’s not easy, but people are trying very hard. One very important thing is to keep everything as clean as possible. This means sweeping up all the food crumbs. Putting all the rubbish and food scraps into special bins that have tight lids. If there are no yummy smells or food left around, the rats won't want to come. Families are also trying to keep their food in strong containers that rats cannot chew through. They also try to block any small holes in their tents or around their sleeping areas so rats can't sneak in. Sometimes, special traps are used to catch the rats and take them far away. But because there are so many, it's a big job!

Learning from Far Away

Even though Gaza is very far away, this story teaches us an important lesson that we can use right here in our villages and homes in Africa. Keeping our homes clean, putting our food away safely, and getting rid of rubbish properly helps us all stay healthy and safe from tiny pests. When we work together in our communities to keep our environment tidy, we create a better, safer place for everyone, especially for all the children. We send our love and wishes for strength to the families in Gaza, hoping they can soon live in safe, healthy homes without these tiny worries. Did you know? Keeping our hands clean by washing them with soap and water is one of the best ways to stop germs from spreading, no matter where we live! Remember, we can all do our part to keep our spaces clean and happy!
summary: Families living in tent camps in a far-off place called Gaza are facing a big problem with many tiny rats spreading and making people sick.
whyMatters: This matters because tiny rats can eat food, spread germs, and bite people, making it unsafe and unhealthy for children and families, especially when they already live in difficult tent homes.

content: Christians pray in quiet gatherings to mark the resurrection of Christ while holding on to hope for survival and peace.

originalTitle: Seriously wounded US airman rescued from Iran, Trump says
title: A Brave Airplane Helper Was Saved!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It’s your Africa Reporter Friend here, ready to share some special news from far, far away. We live in a big, beautiful world, and sometimes things happen that remind us how important it is for people to help each other. Today’s story is about a brave helper who got hurt and was found safe. It’s a story about people caring and working together.

A Brave Helper Gets Hurt

Imagine someone who works with amazing flying machines called airplanes. These people are very brave! They are called 'airmen' or 'airwomen.' They fly big planes high in the sky, sometimes to help people, sometimes to learn new things. Well, one of these brave helpers, an 'airman' from a country called America, got into a bit of trouble. He got very, very hurt. We say he was 'seriously wounded.' This means he had a big injury, much bigger than a scraped knee or elbow. He needed help, and he needed it fast!

What is an Airman?

Let's talk a little more about what an airman does. Think of them like super helpers in the sky! They might fly big cargo planes that carry important things, or sometimes they fly planes that help watch over places to keep people safe. They are very skilled and brave people who spend a lot of time learning how to fly and work with these big machines. When we hear that an airman got hurt, it makes us sad, because we know they are usually busy doing important work. So, finding this airman was a very, very important job. Did you know? Airplanes fly super fast, much faster than any car or train! Some airplanes can even fly higher than the tallest mountains!

A Journey to a Faraway Land

This airman was in a country far away from America, a place called Iran. Our world is made up of many, many countries, like our own wonderful countries here in Africa. Each country is like a different big house on our planet. When someone gets hurt in a faraway place, it can be tricky to find them. Imagine losing your favorite toy when you're visiting a friend's house in another town. It's not as easy to find as when it's at your own home, right? That's a bit like what happened here. The airman was hurt in a place that wasn't his home country, America. Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in the world! Each one has its own special flag and traditions, just like our own countries in Africa.

The Big Search and Rescue!

Because the airman was very hurt and needed help, people started looking for him. This is called a 'search.' Imagine you dropped a small button and everyone in your family helped you look for it. That's a search! The people from America started looking very hard. And do you know what? People from the country where he was, Iran, also started looking! It was like many people were looking for that lost button. Everyone knew someone important needed to be found and helped. When they find someone who is in danger or hurt and bring them to safety, we call that a 'rescue.' A rescue is a truly wonderful thing, showing how much people care. Did you know? Rescue teams sometimes use special dogs with amazing noses to help find people who are lost or in trouble!

Different Countries, One Big Goal

So, we had people from America and people from Iran, both looking for this hurt airman. Even though America and Iran are different countries, and sometimes countries don't always agree on everything, in this case, the goal was simple: find the hurt person. The leader of America, Mr. Trump, told everyone the good news: the airman was found! He was brought to safety. This shows us that even when people or countries are different, they can still work towards helping someone who is in need. It's a very important lesson for all of us, isn't it? Helping others is a super-power we all have. Did you know? Sometimes, different countries work together to send food or medicine to places that need help, or to explore space!

Why Helping is So Important

This story makes us feel happy because it shows that when someone is hurt, people will try their very best to help. It doesn't matter where they are from or what country they belong to. When someone is in trouble, it's a very kind and important thing to reach out and help them. The airman was seriously wounded, but because people looked for him and rescued him, he can now get better. He can go home to his family and friends who must have been very worried. Did you know? Being kind and helping others can make you feel happy too! It's like a warm, fuzzy feeling inside!

Let's All Be Helpers!

So, my fantastic friends, what can we learn from this news? We learn that even from very far away, people care about each other. We learn that helping someone who is hurt or lost is one of the best things you can do. Let’s all remember to be like the people in this story. Let’s be kind. Let’s look out for our friends, our family, and even strangers who might need a hand. You, too, can be a helper every single day! Keep being amazing, and I’ll see you next time with more news from our big, exciting world! Remember to always look for ways to make someone's day a little better. It’s what makes our world such a wonderful place! Goodbye for now!
summary: A brave American airman who was very hurt in a faraway country called Iran has been found and rescued, showing how important it is for people to help each other.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that helping people who are hurt or in danger is very important, even if they are from a different country. It shows how kindness and working together can bring someone home safely.
